commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Paul means by this that he and Barnabas do have this right, but they do not want to exercise it. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: The Lord directed those who preach the gospel to live by the gospel, that is, to maintain at the expense of the faithful that life for which food and clothing are essential.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: 1 Corinthians 9:6 Or I only and Barnabas, have not we the right to forbear working. (See his humility of mind and his soul pure from envy, how he takes care not to conceal him whom he knew to be a partaker with himself in this perfection. ) For if the other things be common, how is not this common. Both they and we are apostles and are free, and have seen Christ, and have exhibited the works of Apostles. Therefore we likewise have a right both to live without working and to be supported by our disciples.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: For there is extant withal an Epistle to the Hebrews under the name of Barnabas-a man sufficiently accredited by God, as being one whom Paul has stationed next to himself in the uninterrupted observance of abstinence: "Or else, I alone and Barnabas, have not we the power of working? "

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
